# Federated Brand Reference

Source: DX-DesignSystem Storybook — Brand Identity/Federated/Foundation

---

## Typography

There are **two font families** for Federated digital products, following brand guidelines:

### Primary: Century Gothic Pro

Used for most headings and UI text.

| Role | Font | Weight |
|------|------|--------|
| Display / H1 | Century Gothic Pro | Bold |
| H2 / Section | Century Gothic Pro | Bold |
| H3 / Subsection | Century Gothic Pro | Regular |
| Body / UI text | Century Gothic Pro | Regular |

### Secondary: Museo Slab

Used only at **700 weight** (no other weights allowed).

| Role | Font | Weight |
|------|------|--------|
| Accent headings, callouts | Museo Slab | **700 only** |

> Never mix NBFC fonts (Proxima Nova) with Federated fonts (Century Gothic / Museo Slab). They are separate brand identities.

---

## Logo

The Federated Insurance logo is a **unified graphic** — it cannot be altered in any way.

### Variations

Multiple formats exist for different media. Choice depends on: available space, content, and target market.

### Logo do's
- Use approved logo variations provided in the asset library
- Maintain required clear space around the logo
- Apply logo on appropriate backgrounds that ensure legibility

### Logo don'ts
- Do not stretch, distort, recolor, or recreate the logo
- Do not add effects (shadows, glows, outlines)
- Do not place on visually busy backgrounds that reduce contrast
- Do not combine the Federated logo with other logos without following co-branding guidelines
- Do not use low-resolution versions

---

## Components (Federated)

### Cards

Three types:

1. **Static Card** — Displays fixed information, no user interaction. Three variations:
   - Primary (with icon)
   - Testimonial
   - Steps

2. **Interactive Card** — User can engage via a clickable link or dynamic behaviour.

3. **Interactive Image-based Card** — Visual asset as the primary element; entire card is a link.

### Card Layouts

Four column layout options: 1-column, 2-column, 3-column, 4-column.
Spacing and padding principles apply consistently across all column counts.

### Card Accessibility Rules
- All interactive cards must be fully keyboard-operable
- Interactive cards must have meaningful accessible names (especially when the whole card is a link)
- Images inside cards need descriptive alt text (unless purely decorative)
- All text, icons, and interactive elements must meet WCAG contrast minimums
- Do not rely solely on color to communicate state — use structural/textual indicators too

### Content Block (Federated)

A structured content section component. See `components.md` for shared usage rules.

### Navigation (Federated)

- **Breadcrumbs** — Same pattern as NBFC; see `components.md`

---

## Consistency Rules

- Federated brand identity is separate from NBFC. Do not apply NBFC colors, typography, or iconography to Federated UI.
- Building brand requires "conscious, coordinated, consistent" application of all guidelines.
- When in doubt about a Federated-specific color or spacing token not listed here, reference the Figma design file: `https://www.figma.com/design/3XHKXYQj9ICLFXtVwRn26G/Fed-Design-System`
